title: hypericum perforatum subsp. veronense
intro: Hypericum perforatum subsp. veronense, also known as St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Hypericum perforatum. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as hyperforin and hypericin. The health benefits of this plant include treating mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and sleep disorders, as well as providing relief from wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Hypericum perforatum subsp. veronense can be found in the form of teas, capsules, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
